details.elaboration: ["Quote: Finally, many animals, in this process, were found sick or injured and due to lack of people on ground - they were left unreported. So we at YODA set up a large scale search and rescue team to go out and identify such animals in pain to treat on site or admit depending upon situation.", "Highest tractability intervention found: 'Individual Rescue And Sanctuary'", "EA Rationale: Direct rescue provides high value to individual animals but is considered less tractable for large-scale change due to its high cost-per-animal and limited scope."]
details.formula: Mapping of charity's intervention types against the EA Intervention Tractability evidence base.

details.elaboration: Quote: 'With your support, in 2020, we made immense progress in scaling up our sick and injured stray animal rehabilitation practice, rescued a large number of animals who were the victims of grave cruelty, and we scaled up our feeding program to cover over 5 Lakh meals for stray animals who were left starving on the road due to the pandemic.'
details.formula: Highest level of self-reported evidence cited in impact metrics

details.calculation: No quantified counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline

details.calculation: Financials with income breakdown are missing.
Mixed portfolio with minority focus on high-neglectedness areas (Farmed Animals: 11%, Wild Animals: 3%, Companion Animals: 86%).
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

metrics.context_qualifier: Provided during the COVID-19 pandemic lockdown for starving stray animals in Maharashtra.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Stray animals would have been left starving on the road due to the pandemic lockdown.

metrics.evidence_quote: With your support, in 2020, we made immense progress in scaling up our sick and injured stray animal rehabilitation practice, rescued a large number of animals who were the victims of grave cruelty, and we scaled up our feeding program to cover over 5 Lakh meals for stray animals who were left starving on the road due to the pandemic.

metrics.context_qualifier: Animals medically rehabilitated at YODA's shelter in 2020.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Homeless animals would have been left to die due to lack of medical facilities or access.

metrics.evidence_quote: 18,254 animals were medically rehabilitated at our shelter in 2020.

metrics.context_qualifier: Success stories of animals rehomed, rehabilitated, or released from YODA's shelter in 2020.
metrics.evidence_quote: Out of those, we have had approx. 17,800 success stories of animals being rehomed or rehabilitated and release.

metrics.context_qualifier: Homeless animals vaccinated by YODA in 2020.
metrics.evidence_quote: In 2020, we have vaccinated 8000 animals and Sterilized 1,822 homeless animals

metrics.context_qualifier: Animals successfully adopted through YODA's program in 2020.
metrics.evidence_quote: In 2020, we have successfully homed 2489 animals

metrics.context_qualifier: Homeless animals sterilized by YODA in 2020.
metrics.evidence_quote: In 2020, we have vaccinated 8000 animals and Sterilized 1,822 homeless animals

metrics.context_qualifier: Retired police dogs from explosive and drug detection teams successfully rehabilitated and rehomed.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Many ill-treated, neglected, or abandoned animals develop serious behavioral issues and are often put down.

metrics.evidence_quote: 50 Retired Police dogs from explosive and drug detection teams were successfully rehabilitated and rehomed

significant_events.source_quote: We organized a robust emergency daily feeding program for thousands of street animals around Maharashtra who were left to fend for themselves during public lockdown periods.
significant_events.summary: Organized a daily feeding program for thousands of street animals in Maharashtra during the COVID-19 lockdown.

significant_events.event_name: Large Scale Search and Rescue Team Setup
significant_events.source_quote: Finally, many animals, in this process, were found sick or injured and due to lack of people on ground - they were left unreported. So we at YODA set up a large scale search and rescue team to go out and identify such animals in pain to treat on site or admit depending upon situation.
significant_events.summary: Established a team to identify and treat sick/injured animals on-site or admit them to the shelter during the pandemic.

significant_events.source_quote: With more individuals working from home, YODA has a good opportunity to carry out large scale fostering programs. We had four times the usual number of individuals who were willing to foster orphaned, injured or sick animals until they were ready for rehoming.
significant_events.summary: Expanded fostering programs during the pandemic, increasing foster parents fourfold for orphaned, injured, or sick animals.

significant_events.event_name: Retired Police Dog Rehabilitation & Adoption Program
significant_events.source_quote: YODA tied up with local police in Maharashtra and Bangalore to take in all retired police dogs for behavioral rehabilitation and adoption.
significant_events.summary: Partnership with local police to rehabilitate and rehome retired police dogs from explosive and drug detection teams.

significant_events.source_quote: The organization started by conducting awareness programs in schools, slums, residential societies and various communities to educate individuals about what to do when they see a stray animal in distress.
significant_events.summary: Conducts awareness programs in schools, slums, and communities to educate about animal protection and welfare.

data.original_text: Youth Organization in Defense of Animals (YODA), Mumbai was born with a vision to create awareness of the plight of stray and abandoned animals in India.
We mainly work with dogs and cats, we neuter and work on first aid cases, adoption, rescues, rehabilitation.
YODA is open 6 days a week for free of cost OPD for homeless animals.

YODA is known in the Mumbai region for providing a wide range of animal welfare activities like rescue, rehabilitation, rehoming, medical aid, background research for adopters, and medical emergencies when required.
The organization actively finds homes for stray dogs and pups and mass sterilizations stray animals in different zones across Mumbai.

The organization started by conducting awareness programs in schools, slums, residential societies and various communities to educate individuals about what to do when they see a stray animal in distress.
YODA's Mission is to develop a community that values and respects animals.
From educating youth to emergency services for stray animals, we now positively help and impact approx. 1000 animals each month across Maharashtra.

We encourage communities to choose adoption over purchase of animals, provide emergency mental and physical medical rehabilitation for animals in distress, educate urban, semi- urban and rural communities on the subject and work towards overall prevention of cruelty.

With your support, in 2020, we made immense progress in scaling up our sick and injured stray animal rehabilitation practice, rescued a large number of animals who were the victims of grave cruelty, and we scaled up our feeding program to cover over 5 Lakh meals for stray animals who were left starving on the road due to the pandemic.
YODA gets over 100 animals successfully adopted each month.
YODA Animal Welfare has been recognized at the Being Human Awards for its incredible work in rescue and rehabilitation.

name: Youth Organisation in Defence of Animals
